queue.title: Auto-slim gate for SESSION_*.md files
queue.description: (added ~s1872): The token-budget-estimator already detects over-budget files and auto-creates wq items. Add a pre-commit hook check that warns (non-blocking) when any SESSION_*.md edit pushes a file over the 3000-token threshold, catching bloat at commit time rather than waiting for the next audit cycle.

queue.title: TODO followup: // todo-scan.mjs — TODO tracker management for 27-todo-scan.sh
Auto-update BRIEFING.md hook count in pre-session hook
queue.description: BRIEFING.md hook count has drifted 3 times (wq-917, wq-922, and a218-2). Add a pre-session hook step that counts hooks via ls and updates BRIEFING.md inline if the stated count differs from actual. Eliminates drift entirely. Small scope: one sed/awk replacement in a pre-hook, tested with bash -n.

queue.title: Add threshold=4 boundary test for auto-promote
queue.description: session-context.test.mjs tests 0-3 pending items but lacks a test for exactly 4 pending (the boundary where auto-promote should NOT fire). Add intTestAutoPromote4Pending() with 4 pending items and 5 fresh ideas, asserting no promotion occurs. Catches future threshold regression.

queue.title: Group 4: absorb 3 E-posthook hooks into 36-e-session-posthook_E.sh
queue.description: d075 Group 4: absorb 36-picker-compliance_E.sh, 17-engagement-log.sh, 26-engage-blockers.sh into the existing E posthook dispatcher. -3 hooks (59→56). Follow the same absorption pattern as Groups 1-3/6-8. Also get E posthook under 300 lines (currently ~372, needs slim after absorption).

queue.title: Add tests for note-fallback.mjs and truncation-recovery.mjs
queue.description: d076 batch 1: add test files for hooks/lib/note-fallback.mjs and hooks/lib/truncation-recovery.mjs. Each needs 3+ tests covering happy path, missing/malformed input, and module-specific edge case. These are small, self-contained modules ideal for a first batch.

queue.title: Clean up session:9999 artifacts in directives.json compliance history
queue.description: R#342 code review finding: directives.json compliance.metrics contains 21 entries with session:9999 — likely from a test run or broken session counter. These pollute compliance history and skew nudge calculations. Fix: replace 9999 values with the nearest plausible session number based on surrounding history entries, or remove them. Also verify directive-audit.py correctly receives SESSION_NUM (it does — confirmed reading the code).
